summary: Gruber offers rapid-fire reactions to Apple's March 2019 'Show Time' event, covering Apple Card, Apple News Plus, Apple Arcade, and Apple TV Plus. He's sharply critical of Apple Card's 'low interest rate' claim, calling rates of 13-24% APR usurious. He finds Apple News Plus underwhelming and questions whether digital magazines are still relevant. Apple Arcade gets the highest praise as the most cohesive announcement. The TV Plus segment draws the harshest critique — a parade of celebrities that felt uncharacteristically un-Apple, leaving too many questions unanswered about pricing and content.
key_points: Apple Card's advertised 'low interest rates' of 13-24% APR are standard industry usury, not a consumer-friendly innovation
Apple Arcade was the standout announcement — clear value proposition, well-presented, and makes Google's cloud gaming pitch look weak
The TV Plus segment felt like a presentation from Google or Amazon, not Apple — too many celebrities, not enough substance
Multiple announcements (Arcade, TV Plus) left out pricing, a frustrating omission across the event
Apple News Plus reprises the failed Newsstand concept and doesn't even include full Wall Street Journal access
money_quotes: What a crock of shit this 'low interest rates' line is. Those interest rates are usury, right in line with the rest of the credit card industry.
24% interest ought to be criminal, and 13% is not 'low'.
Are magazines still a thing? Didn't Apple go down this same path with Newsstand back when the iPad first launched?
This was the most cohesive announcement of the day. Easy to understand what it is, why you'd want it, and what the value proposition is.
I think this is going to be a big hit, and it makes Google's 'we've got games running on our servers' thing from last week look a bit silly.
The whole TV Plus segment felt like a presentation from another company, like Google or Amazon, not Apple.
It feels like Apple was starstruck.
We started the day with a lot of unanswered questions about Apple's original content strategy and we're ending the day with most of those questions still unanswered.
key_insight: Apple's services event was uneven — Apple Arcade nailed the formula of clarity and value, while Apple Card overpromised and TV Plus felt like a starstruck company imitating its less disciplined competitors.
